mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates directly increase corporate borrowing costs and discount rates, suppressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean in >65% of observations when FEDFUNDS > 4.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid three-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in >70% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to predict direction with accuracy >55% in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized collapse in consumer and housing demand, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal shows no incremental predictive power over single-series models, or 60-day forward returns remain positive in >60% of co-declining periods.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing and utility output, which supports corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for INDPRO growth turns negative during mid-cycle expansion periods, or 20-day forward returns are not significantly higher than baseline when INDPRO increases.

mechanism_narrative: Year-over-year consumer price index growth exceeding 4.0% triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity multiples and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive in >65% of observations when CPIAUCSL YoY exceeds 4.0%, or the threshold signal yields a negative Rank IC over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market expansion, which boosts consumer spending and corporate profits, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal fails to predict positive returns with >58% accuracy, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical difference from baseline during co-movement periods.

mechanism_narrative: Positive quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects broad economic expansion that sustains corporate earnings trajectories, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.01 over 3-year rolling windows, or 60-day forward returns are negative in >60% of positive GDP growth quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5% signals a sharp contraction in manufacturing activity, which reduces near-term earnings expectations and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in >65% of triggered instances, or the threshold signal yields a negative Rank IC over any 2-year rolling window.
